Bits, bytes and nibbles: An electronic forum on computers in journalism debuts

Not explicitly provided Not explicitly provided October 1992
Data & DatabasesEducationJournalism & Newsroom

The University of Louisville established CARR-L, an online forum for journalists and educators to discuss computer-assisted reporting techniques including databases, graphics, and electronic data research. The forum serves as a community hub for sharing resources, software reviews, and job opportunities in computer-assisted journalism.

The University of Louisville launched an online forum for journalists and educators focused on using computers in journalism. Called Computer-Assisted Research and Reporting List (CARR-L), it covers topics such as text processing, graphics, database searching, and computer communication. The forum facilitates discussions on finding electronic state-level data, hurricane research, software reviews, and job announcements.

For more information, contact Elliot Parker from Central Michigan University, who is one of CARR-L's curators.
